refnamediv.refpurpose: read and write live virtual machine clipboard data

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The VBoxManage clipboard command reads and writes live clipboard data for a running virtual machine. The command uses standard input and standard output by default, which makes it suitable for shell pipelines.
Clipboard data transfer requires the virtual machine to be running and the shared clipboard mode to allow the requested direction. The command does not enable shared clipboard access by itself.

refsect2.title: Copy Host Data to the Virtual Machine Clipboard
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard copy command copies data from host standard input to the live clipboard of the virtual machine. Use shell pipes or input redirection to copy data from a host file.

listitem.para: Specifies the MIME type for the clipboard data. The default is text/plain;charset=utf-8.
Treats the input as binary data. If no explicit MIME type was specified, the command uses application/octet-stream.

refsect2.screen: $ echo "hello from host" | VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" copy

refsect2.title: Paste Virtual Machine Clipboard Data to the Host
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard paste command reads data from the live clipboard of the virtual machine and writes it to host standard output. It does not synthesize a paste keystroke in the guest. Use shell redirection to write the clipboard payload to a host file, for example VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" paste > foo.txt.

If the command would print multiline text to an interactive terminal, it displays a safe preview on standard error and asks for confirmation before writing the clipboard payload to standard output. Redirect or pipe standard output to avoid this prompt.

listitem.para: Requires the clipboard data to have the specified MIME type.

Waits up to the specified number of milliseconds for new guest clipboard data before writing it to the host. If no matching data arrives before the timeout, the command exits with an error and writes no clipboard payload to standard output.

refsect2.screen: $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" paste > foo.txt $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" paste --wait=10000 > foo.txt

refsect2.title: List Clipboard Formats
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard formats command lists MIME formats currently available from the live virtual machine clipboard.

refsect2.title: Reset Clipboard State
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard reset command resets live clipboard data and transfer state for the virtual machine console.

refsect2.title: Listen for Clipboard Events
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard listen command listens for live clipboard events from the virtual machine console. It prints event metadata only; it does not print clipboard payloads.

listitem.para: Specifies a comma-separated list of events to listen for. Valid values are source, format, data, transfer, error, mode, and file-transfer-mode. The source-changed, format-changed, and data-changed aliases are also accepted for the corresponding changed events. The default is source,format,data,transfer,error,mode,file-transfer-mode.
Specifies the event output format. JSON output is written as one JSON object per line.
Stops listening after the specified number of milliseconds.
Stops listening after printing the specified number of events.
